entry.title: How to implement additional booking periods (More than 12) in SAC and Datasphere?
entry.content:
Hi all,

we are currently working on a reporting using SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere with data coming from S/4HANA. 

In S/4HANA it is possible to work with additional booking periods (More than the typical 12 month booking periods). We have the requirement to report these additional booking periods using SAP Analytics Cloud and Datasphere. As there is barely any information or blogs regarding this and no standard solution available in SAC/Datasphere, we were wondering if someone worked on a similar solution before or if there are any best practices for this kind of implementation.

Any help is much appreciated!

Best regards

entry.title: What’s new in SAP Convergent Mediation 9.3 by DigitalRoute
entry.content:
New Release

Public

January 2025

We are excited to announce the release of SAP Convergent Mediation 9.3!

This release brings several updates designed to optimize productivity, enhance scalability and performance, and strengthen security and compliance, including features focused on refining usability. 

In MediationZone 9.3, we have further advanced the platform's ability to handle real-time and batch data processing, deepened integration with major cloud platforms, and strengthened security features to protect sensitive data. These updates allow you to scale operations more effectively, streamline processes, and maintain secure, compliant environments. 

Some of the key features are:

 

Kafka Enhancements

This feature encompasses a complete refactoring of the Kafka agents, introducing a new set of collector agents and a real-time forwarder agent with support for subscribing to multiple topics per agent, with wildcard functionality.  

Improved support for batch processes also enhances efficiency when handling large volumes of data. The new agents enable scale-out capabilities with automatic rebalancing, ensuring that multiple workflows can efficiently manage and process data in parallel.  

The new agents bring significant usability and efficiency improvements in configuration, management, and operation, and enable you to seamlessly integrate Kafka with MediationZone for optimized data processing and management, see Kafka Agents.

 

Change Transport System (CTS+)

In this release a new, streamlined, way of transporting configuration exports and imports between landscapes is introduced (for example, moving configurations from Development to Test landscape), which aligns with adjacent products in the SAP BRIM stack. For more details, see SAP CTS+ Integration User's Guide.

 

Reference Data Management Support for HANA

This release extends the database support for the Reference Data Management GUI to also support SAP HANA database. Customers can now search and amend their HANA database tables (e.g, reference, audit, source, target) directly in this Web GUI, enhancing the operational efficiency and way of working. For further details, see Reference Data Management User's Guide.

 

Support Google Cloud Secret Manager for Secrets and Security profiles

Securing sensitive information, such as passwords, certificates, and keys, is a fundamental requirement in today’s digital landscape. The integration with Google Cloud Secret Manager in this release provides centralized secret management, allowing users to securely store and retrieve secrets while associating them with specific environments.

This granular level of control ensures that only authorized individuals and systems can access the necessary credentials, offering a robust enhancement to overall security measures.

See Google Secret Manager Profile for more information.

 

New Features

Usability

  • Desktop Search
  • Log Filter
  • Support for “wfexport” and “wfimport” mzsh Commands
  • MZSH Command For Shutting Down Desktop and Legacy Desktop

New Connectors

  • New Kafka Agents
  • Amazon S3 Agents in Real-Time Workflows
  • Amazon SQS Agents
  • APN Processing Agent
  • SAP CC REST Agent

MariaDB Support

  • Supported Agents for MariaDB
  • Additional APL Functions and Event Notifications Support MariaDB

Integration and Security

  • User Security Improvements
  • SAP CTS+ Integration
  • Reference Data Management Supports SAP HANA

Authentication and Monitoring

  • Google Secret Manager
  • System Statistics

Enhancements

  • followRedirects Field for RequestCycle UDR in HTTP/2 Agents
  • HTTP/2 Client Agent Support Additional OAuth Settings

 

More information 

  • SAP Product Page and Product Video
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.3 Release
  • SAP Convergent Mediation 9.3 Documentation
  • SAP CM 9.3 Composite SAP Noteand SAP CM Help Portal page
  • SAP CM Mainstream Maintenance Information

entry.title: Re: Fiori app Mass change of sales orders returns error '...
entry.content: issue resolved: the issue was caused by a program that made an attempt to use the UI while running in the background, which resulted in an error. By adding a check in the program on the system variable sy-batch = X (which means the process is running in the background), this can be avoided.

entry.title: Re: SAP Public cloud (Grow) - option to develop simple APPs
entry.content: That is not correct. I have worked in 4 S/4HANA Cloud Public projects so far (I'm working on other 2 right now). You can access such systems using Eclipse without any issues. Another different thing is the type of developments that you can create. We need to use ABAP Cloud, which is very limited, I must say. But we can still create Classes, CDS Views, BADis and some other stuff.

entry.title: Integrate SAP Project and Resource Management with 3rd Party Human Experience Management Solution
entry.content:
Collaborative effort of Pavalavel Kanagavel

This blog post will provide detailed information on how to Integrate SAP Project and Resource Management (SPRM) earlier known as Cloud for Projects (C4P) with 3rd party Human Experience Management (HXM) Solution. Here you will find useful informative links to help you integrate SPRM with 3rd party HXM solution in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Introduction

Let’s take a scenario, yours is a professional services industry and you have recently migrated to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition from your Legacy System, ECC, On-Premise or S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition systems of SAP. In a professional services industry, resource management is an integral part and your profitability, Revenue calculation and Margin analysis is closely dependent on the resource identification, allocation, and staffing. Till the time you went live with S/4HANA Cloud Public edition, you were running/operating your day-to-day business with a combination of SAP and Non-SAP Solutions. You might be having an employee central system which can either be an SAP solution like the Success Factors (SF) or any other 3rd Party Non-SAP Solution. Our blog mainly concentrates on the 2nd scenario where the employee central is a non-SAP 3rd Party HXM Solution.

If Success Factors (SF) was your employee central solution before migration, then you would be able to directly integrate SF with SPRM via Master Data Integration (MDI) service by following the steps explained in the administration guide of Resource Management for Projects (6JO) scope item. SAP Project and Resource Management is available as a service on SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)

If you were using a non-SAP 3rd party HXM solution as your employee central. After the migration, would need to bring all your resource related information from that non-SAP 3rd party HXM solution into SAP Project and Resource Management Solution to successfully run your business. SAP has given the flexibility to achieve this requirement in two different ways based on the services the customer is already entitled for and the subscriptions that he holds. the two suggested routes to connect / integrate non-SAP 3rd Party HXM solution with SPRM are as listed below.

  1.          SAP Integration suite.
  2.          S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Below is the detailed write up about the two use cases / scenarios where in you would like to use your SAP Integration suite to establish the connection between non-SAP system and MDI, or you would like to use your S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system as a single source system for your employee central. Both the use cases have been explained in detail in the later part of the documentation. By following the steps suggested in the respective use cases, you would be able to achieve your intended goal of getting your resource related information from non-SAP 3rd party HXM Solution to SPRM.

Use Case 1: SAP Integration suite

SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) that helps you quickly integrate on-premise and cloud-based processes, services, applications, events, and data. It comes with inbuilt /pre-configured integration scenarios which can readily be consumed, and you also have the feasibility to build your own custom integration scenarios. This comes with an additional license and once you are entitled for this service, you can build your own integration scenarios.

As part of your business plan, along with S/4HANA Cloud Public edition if you have also subscribed for SAP Integration suite for addressing your integration requirements, then you can make use of the same Integration suite to build an integration between 3rd party non-SAP HXM solution and SPRM via MDI.

SAP Cloud Integration version 6.27.**, one of the capabilities of SAP Integration Suite, comes with addition of Master Data Integration – MDI – adapter, to integrate your scenarios with SAP Master Data Integration service. This enables the integration scenarios, especially 3rd party integrations, to communicate with SAP Master Data Integration service via SAP Cloud Integration through the MDI Adapter for their master data synchronisation.

Before working with MDI adapter, the relevant subscription process to SAP Master Data Integration (MDI) service should be completed and understanding of One Domain Model (ODM) entities is required.

You can refer to the Blog that clearly describes about the 3rd party integrations with help of Integration suite + MDI/MDO.

Use Case 2: S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

As part of your business plan, if you only have the S/4HANA Cloud Public edition license and you do not plan to purchase /get the entitlement for SAP Integration suite for the only purpose of getting the resource information from an 3rd party non-SAP HXM solution, then you can keep your S4HC system as the single source of truth for the employee information. With the help of existing available API’s you can establish a connection between 3rd party non-SAP HXM solution and S4HC to replicate the employee information.

The resource information can be replicated to SAP Project and Resource Management solution by keeping S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as your source system for all data replications. Once you have workforce person information in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can replicate the resource information to SPRM via the inbuilt integration scenarios and here also we use MDI.

The replication of workforce person information from 3rd Party Non-SAP HXM solution to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition can be achieved via the available API’s. the step-by-step process is explained below.

Pre-requisite is Resource Management for Projects (6JO) scope item is already in scope and activated. 

  1. Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems.

This API based inbound service enables you to replicate the basic master data of your workforce, both employees and external workers, from an external HR system to your SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system.

You can find the API documentation at Replicate Basic Master Data for Workforce from External HR Systems. All the nodes are explained separately and some use cases are described in detail. Technical information is available at API WORKFORCEPERSONMASTERDATAREPLI.

  1. Replicate Workforce Availability information to SPRM via APIs provided by SPRM.

The detailed information about the Workforce Availability API can be found in this link

  1. Replicate Workforce qualification and Work experience to SPRM via APIs provided by SPRM.

The detailed information about the Project Experience API which captures Qualification and Work experience can be found in this link

The above mentioned APIs for resource management (Workforce availability & Project Experience) are available on the SAP Business Accelerator Hub under https://api.sap.com/package/SAPS4HANACloudForProjectsResourceManagement .

You can set-up the API access and start using/accessing the APIs as a technical user with the required authorizations. There are certain pre-requisites that have be fulfilled before accessing the resource management APIs as listed below.

  • You have set up your global account and subaccount. For more information, see Managing Global Accounts Using the Cockpit and Managing Subaccounts Using the Cockpit.
  • You are assigned the Administrator role for the global account.
  • You have created a Cloud Foundry space. For more information, see Managing Spaces.
  • You have subscribed to the resource management capability in SAP Project and Resource Management. For more information, see Onboarding section of Administration Guide for Resource Management in SAP Project and Resource Management.

Once all the pre-requisites are met, you would need to carry out the further steps like

  • Creating a Service Instance: Create an instance of the Resource Management - API service.
  • Creating a Service Key: Create a service key to get the credentials for accessing the resource management APIs.
  • Generating an Access Token Using Client Credential Authentication: Generate an access token and access the APIs.
  • Generating an Access Token Using X.509 Client Certificate Authentication: Generate an access token and access the APIs.

Detailed information and set by step procedure of setting up API access has been clearly explained in this link.

 Conclusion

This blog post should help you to understand in detail how to replicate workforce information from 3rd party non-SAP HXM Solution using either the SAP Integration Suite or the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with the API’s listed in SAP Business Accelerator Hub.

Thanks for reading this blog post, hopefully the blog post was informative.

entry.title: SAP ALM Roadshow LAC 2025 - Mexico Edition
entry.content:
¡Únase a Nosotros para el SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC 2025 en México! 📅

Después de exitosos roadshows en São Paulo (Brasil) el 11 de noviembre de 2024 🇧🇷 y Buenos Aires (Argentina) el 19 de noviembre de 2024 🇦🇷, estamos emocionados de traer el SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC a Monterrey (México) el 29 de enero de 2025 🇲🇽. ¡Este evento será una oportunidad fantástica para aprender sobre las últimas novedades en la Gestión del Ciclo de Vida de Aplicaciones Cloud de SAP (Cloud ALM) y descubrir herramientas y estrategias que pueden ayudarle a optimizar sus proyectos! 🚀📈

Ya sea que haya asistido a uno de nuestros roadshows anteriores o que se una a nosotros por primera vez, este evento está cargado de valiosos conocimientos, talleres prácticos y oportunidades de networking que no querrá perderse. 📚💼🤝

¿Qué es SAP Cloud ALM? 🌐
La Gestión del Ciclo de Vida de Aplicaciones Cloud de SAP (Cloud ALM) ofrece un conjunto integral de herramientas en la nube para apoyar todo el ciclo de vida de sus aplicaciones, desde el desarrollo hasta la implementación, el monitoreo y la optimización continua. Con SAP Cloud ALM, las organizaciones pueden mejorar la eficiencia, reducir riesgos y garantizar la ejecución exitosa de proyectos, todo mientras fomentan la colaboración entre equipos. 🤖✨

¿Por qué asistir al SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC? 🤔💡
🔍Perspectivas Exclusivas sobre las Herramientas de SAP Cloud ALM: Obtenga un conocimiento profundo de las últimas innovaciones y estrategias de SAP Cloud ALM para mejorar el ciclo de vida de sus aplicaciones.
🌎Soluciones Específicas para la Región: Aprenda cómo SAP Cloud ALM aborda los desafíos únicos que enfrentan las empresas en América Latina, basándose en el éxito de nuestros roadshows en Brasil y Argentina.
🤝Oportunidades de Networking: Conéctese con expertos de SAP, compañeros y líderes de la industria en la región. Comparta experiencias, haga preguntas y establezca relaciones con otros profesionales.

Temas Clave que Cubriremos: 📝 En el SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC de este año, cubriremos una amplia gama de temas cruciales que le ayudarán a optimizar su entorno SAP:

🔧SAP Cloud ALM para RISE con SAP y Estrategias de Clean Core Support: Comprenda cómo SAP Cloud ALM se integra con RISE con SAP y apoya las estrategias de Clean Core, permitiendo migraciones más fluidas y optimizaciones del sistema. 
🤖Implementación de la Empresa Inteligente con SAP Cloud ALM: Aprenda cómo SAP Cloud ALM puede ser aprovechado para implementar una Empresa Inteligente, permitiendo que las empresas alineen sus sistemas SAP con estrategias de transformación digital a prueba de futuro.
🧪 Automatización de Pruebas con Tricentis: Descubra cómo la avanzada automatización de pruebas de Tricentis puede acelerar sus ciclos de proyectos SAP, reducir riesgos y mejorar la garantía de calidad en toda la organización.
🤖🔧Optimización de Operaciones Combinando IA y SAP Cloud ALM: Explore cómo las soluciones impulsadas por IA dentro de SAP Cloud ALM pueden mejorar su eficiencia operativa, reducir tiempos de inactividad y mejorar la toma de decisiones. 
📊💡Aprovechamiento de SAP Focused Run para Capacidades de Monitoreo Avanzadas: Obtenga información sobre cómo usar SAP Focused Run para habilitar monitoreo avanzado, análisis en tiempo real y resolución proactiva de problemas en su entorno SAP. 
🔄Transición de SAP Solution Manager a SAP Cloud ALM: Obtenga una guía paso a paso sobre cómo realizar la transición de SAP Solution Manager a SAP Cloud ALM con éxito, incluidos los beneficios clave y las mejores prácticas para asegurar una migración sin problemas.

Detalles del Evento: 
📆Fecha: 29 de enero de 2025 
📌Ubicación: Ave. Ricardo Margain 335, Torre 1 - Piso 16; Col. Valle del Campestre, San Pedro Garza García, N.L., México - CP 66265
⏰Hora: 10:00 a.m. - 3:00 p.m. (GMT-3)

¡Regístrese Ahora!

‌‌️‌‌Los cupos son limitados y no querrá perderse esta valiosa experiencia de aprendizaje. Regístrese ahora para asegurar su lugar en el SAP Cloud ALM Roadshow LAC 2025 en México.

Esperamos darle la bienvenida el 29 de enero de 2025 y compartir cómo SAP Cloud ALM puede transformar su negocio. 🏢💼🚀 Manténgase atento a más actualizaciones, anuncios de oradores y agendas detalladas del evento. ¡Nos vemos en México! 👋

#SAPCloudALM #ALM #ALMSummit #SAPSummit #SAPEvents #SolmanTransition

entry.title: Settlement of an Additional agreement party for Rail orders in S/4 HANA Transportation Management
entry.content:
This blog post will help readers, customers, and SAP TM users familiarize themselves with the SAP Transportation Management’s Charge management functionality for Additional payments in S/4 HANA 2023.

In this blog post I will be shedding light only on a peculiar process related to a Rail Journey of carrying out additional payments to parties apart from the carriers who execute the journey. The process may vary on the business needs and processes.

You can read more about SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management -Settlement in the Application Help for SAP S/4HANA

Settlement | SAP Help Portal

Note: I have carried out the demonstration based on my personal tests, observations, insights in S/4HANA 2023 release with relevant screen shot from an internal system for ease of understanding. There’s no relevance or relation to any external source.

Use Case


Use case is for both the customers in Transportation & Logistics Industry (T&L) as well as shippers from Retail, CPG, manufactures etc. operating via rail and have to pay Additional fees to Reload/Train junctions/Diversion fees etc. over and above the contracted charges to more than one Rail carrier incurred as part of Rule 11 pricing.

Assumption: 

The customer is a Manufacturing company and has implemented SAP S/4HANA 2023 and uses embedded SAP Transportation Management.

They have yearly contracts with the multiple rail carriers to move certain capacities on a given corridor/ route. Alternatively, they also have to pay fixed charges to reload location as the reloads are managed by external parties and incurs sundry expenses as part of handling fees. Contract in the SAP TM world should be referred as FRA (Freight Agreement).

Challenge:

 

  • SAP S/4HANA 2022 and lower releases didn’t support payment via Additional Agreement parties for additional expenses incurred apart for the contracted charges in case of rail mode of transport for Rule 11 scenarios.
  • A service order which is an additional object was one of the options to carry out the additional payments and involved a cumbersome task to process it manually or an enhancement to automate the service order creation and associated settlement.
  • In the absence of std functionality, an added human intervention led to delayed or missed payments.

Solution:

SAP TM now has an out of the box solution to enable charge calculation and settlement for Additional agreement parties as of part of BP in the freight order where in stage level charge calculation is activated for rail scenarios. This eliminates the need to create an additional document i.e. the service order. (*Depending on the scenarios, Service orders can be leveraged and its significance should be evaluated on a case-by-case basis)

Note: There are multiple ways in which the additional agreement party can be determined be it via std partner determination profile config or via an available BADI, and purely based on the business rule.

Scenario:


A manufacturer is moving goods from its facility to a customer and routed via Reload location where in the Reload location is operated by an external party. The manufacturer is entitled to pay a fixed amount to the Reload location for the services offered by the reload and consumed by the manufacturer.

Let’s move directly at an instance in time where a rail FRO is created and we have the default route determined with 2 active stages and associated stage level carriers along with Reload BP as the Additional agreement party.

We have valid default routes, Freight agreement i.e. 2 for rail carriers and 1 for reload party.

We start with an assumption that Order management, planning, Charge management and settlement configs are in place as the focus is not on setting up the system but on functionality walk-through.

Pictorial representation of the demonstrated scenario:

Basic rail process flow (Representation purpose only)

Step 1:  Rail FRO is created with stage level carrier.

Step 2 : Reload party BP added as  Additional agreement party.


As mentioned earlier there are multiple ways to determine a BP for Additional agreement party. For demonstration purpose I have added the BP manually and executing/invoicing carriers are determined from the route.
 
Step 3: Once the charge calculation is successful, you can observe that system has fetched the agreements successfully for the two invoicing carriers and reload BP.

Note: Charge structure and associated charges are for representation purpose and are fictitious in nature.

 Step 4:  Let us create freight settlement document for each of the Carrier and Additional agreement party.

The screenshot above demonstrates that system was able to initiate FSD creation for all of the three applicable payable parties.
 The screenshot above demonstrates that no errors were observed during freight settlement document creation process and we can proceed with posting for accruals.

Step 5: Let us observe that all of the three freight settlement documents were posted successfully with visibly no errors observed from an end-to-end process execution stand point.   

Note:
With the introduction of this functionality we can eliminate the introduction of service order which is an additional object and try to keep the process simple and reduce enhancement where were applicable. Some exceptions may apply based on business scenarios and should be evaluated accordingly.


The demonstration in this blog post doesn’t focus on the configs to setup the system nor does it highlight all of the business rules associated with different industry sectors for the carrier payments.

Hope this article helps all of us understand and simplify the payment process associated within the Rail industry in the SAP S/4HANA Transportation Management world. There may be multiple different ways in which a contract may be negotiated between two parties, in this demonstration, I chose to keep it generic and simple.

Thank you for taking out time and effort to read this.

Feedback/comments in any form will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Sap Cloud for customer V1 - Create Report with negated employee selection
entry.content:
Hello folks ,

We want to provide our endusers with a report that shows orders from their customers that were not created by them (logged in user). An easy task, i thought....

I created a report with a "created by" selection. There i was able to select the relativ selection "logged in user".
With that selection the report shows every order, that was created by current logged in user. As describe above , we have the demand to show "the opposite" and so i tried to negated the relative selection with a "!" as prefix. Turns out that this does not work relative selection.

May you have any creative ideas to satisfy the enduser demand?

entry.title: BSP: Problem displaying icon using ITERATOR
entry.content:
Hi!

   I included a column to display icon but I have a problem with the presentation. The icons are not displayed correctly.

Thanks!
The attached shows the logic used.

Class ITERATOR

Method : IF_HTMLB_TABLEVIEW_ITERATOR~RENDER_CELL_START

            WHEN 'CONTROL'.
              ASSIGN COMPONENT p_column_key OF STRUCTURE <fs> TO <l_field>.
              if <l_field> is assigned .
                wf_text = <l_field> .
              endif .
              if wf_text eq '@0A@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Triste.png'.
              elseif wf_text eq '@09@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Media.png'.
              elseif wf_text eq '@08@'.
                figure_ctrl = 'Feliz.png'.
              endif.
              icon_ctrl = CL_BSP_MIMES=>SAP_ICON( id = figure_ctrl ).
              p_replacement_bee = CL_HTMLB_IMAGE=>FACTORY( id = p_cell_id src = icon_ctrl ).

BSP (Layout and MIMEs)

entry.title: What is SAP Asset Performance Management (APM)?
entry.content:
SAP Asset Performance Management (APM) is a SaaS solution from SAP which is meant to be a turnkey solution to enhance the S4 HANA Asset Management solution, previously known as SAP Plant Maintenance.

With SAP APM existing customers can transition from the traditional Reactive, Proactive, Improvement maintenance to the upcoming Predictive, Prescriptive and Reliability Centered maintenance.

Being a cloud solution, SAP APM gets regular updates. These updates bring improvements to the existing functionality as well as new functionalities as they are released. Major releases are every 3 months, whereas minor releases could be as frequent as every month. You could also check the link What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management to know more about the released functionalities.

SAP APM is a cloud solution, but it can integrate both with SAP Cloud and On-Premise solutions for Asset Management. It has a subscription model, and the pricing depends on the usage. SAP APM has embedded IoT to store time series data, and it can also be used for device connectivity. SAP APM also comes with a lot of public APIs which could be very useful to query the data or integrate to generate time series data coming from a 3rd party system.

Fig 1- A very simplistic diagram of data exchange between different SAP systems

 

SAP APM currently has the following major components apart from the Asset Master Data replicated from S4-

  • Asset Health: This component enables Predictive Maintenance.
  • Asset Reliability Engineering: This component enables Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) and Failure Mode Effect Analysis (FMEA).
  • Asset Risk Assessment: This component enables identification of critical assets.
  • Asset Performance Analysis: This component consists of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) which enables ease of creation of reports and dashboards and Failure Curve Analytics (FCA).
  • Industrial Systems: This component is the latest application in SAP APM and it can be used to define systems, which could be a combination of technical objects from different hierarchies.

 

I’ll try to elaborate a little on the different components I mentioned above.

  • Asset Health: With Asset Health you can define Rules on Indicators (measuring points replicated from S4 or defined locally to store calculations) of equipment to compare values and generate Alerts or Notifications if a certain condition is met (for ex. temperature above a threshold or differential pressure below a certain value). You can also view the trend of the time series data and predict failures based on the historical data for a group of equipment. You can also view and compare inspection data of the fixed assets and make decisions on their condition.

 

Fig 2- Indicator trend over time

 

  • Asset Reliability Engineering: You can identify critical equipment using Risk & Criticality and perform  Reliability Centered Maintenance (RCM) for a class of equipment under a certain operating context. Failure data can help you in defining Recommendations which could be sent to S4 as Maintenance Plans and Task Lists. You can also perform Failure Mode & Effect Analysis (FMEA).

 

Fig 3- An example of an RCM assessment

 

  • Asset Risk Assessment: You can identify critical equipment using Risk & Criticality. You can define templates which can then be used to perform risk and criticality assessments on the technical objects. This criticality can be updated in the ABC indicator of the technical objects.

 

Fig 4- An example of a R&C assessment

 

  • Asset Performance Analysis: Using SAC customers may create custom dashboards with various interactive reports, charts and tables can be generated to visualize the data. It may also be used for Failure Curve Analytics (FCA) to create groups of technical objects and then train models to predict the probability of failure for technical objects.

 

Fig 5- Failure curve of an equipment

 

  • Industrial Systems: This component enables definition and structuring of Industrial systems, which could be a combination of plant, functional locations and equipment from different hierarchies with different aspects such as function, product and location. This can give customers another way to define data which perform a function despite being in separate hierarchies in S4. An example could be firefighting system containing fire hydrants, water sprinkler, inert gas system, fire water pump, fire alarm and portable fire extinguishers. Another example could be fuel oil system of a ship which may contain bunker tanks, pipelines, purifiers, settling and service tanks, heaters, filters, fuel oil pumps, fuel nozzles, low fuel oil temperature and pressure alarms etc.

 

Fig 6- A generic example of an Industrial System in SAP APM

 

SAP APM is already being used or being implemented by some of the biggest O&G and Rail customers and customers in other industries. These partners are also contributing  heavily to various innovations using Customer Influence Requests (CIR).

I hope I was able to provide a very basic overview of SAP APM.

Stay tuned! In my upcoming blogs I’ll cover the benefits of SAP APM and why it should be implemented.

You may also read my other blogs-

SAP APM in Maritime Industry

entry.title: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: CSRF Token troubleshooting with S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Good day fellow CAPpers,

Today I'll share one of the lessons I've learned working with CAP, something that may not be clear in the current tutorials or documentation. It's about CSRF (Cross-site Request Forgery) errors when communicating with an S/4HANA Cloud system. Not only that, the communication was working to READ entities, but not for CREATE.

 

Prerequisites

Your SAP Business Technology Platform have is correctly configured to communicate with your S/4HANA Cloud system. The Communication Arrangement (System, Scenario, User) is all set up and it's working to READ information from the S/4HANA Cloud.

 

The Problem

After setting up all the communication, it seems that it was ready to work on BTP.

  1. The app was working locally with the mocked Business Partner imported service.
  2. The Communication was set up with S/4HANA Cloud and Destination correctly set up.
  3. I deployed the application and managed to READ/GET Business Partners.
  4. I could successfully CREATE/POST new Business Partners using Postman !!!
  5. Finally, when I tried to CREATE/POST new Business Partners on the deployed CAP application, I received a CSRF Token error. 

To debug this error, I set up a Hybrid Development, which I highly recommend when testing for development external services.

Turns out that there's a Cookie issue with the communication with S/4HANA Cloud. The CSRF token, rather than going as a header itself (x-csrf-token), it must be set inside a Cookie. I out found about this on a StackOverflow post, which led me to a journey where I finally managed to make it work.

const cds = require('@sap/cds');
const { executeHttpRequest } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client');

// ...

let filledHeaders = {};
const fetchHeader = { "x-csrf-token": "fetch" };
const result = await executeHttpRequest({
    destinationName: cds.requires.API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.credentials.destination
  },{
    method: 'GET',
    url: cds.requires.API_BUSINESS_PARTNER.credentials.path + '/A_BusinessPartner?$top=1',
    headers: fetchHeader
  });
filledHeaders['Cookie'] = result.headers["set-cookie"];
filledHeaders['x-csrf-token'] = result.headers['x-csrf-token'];
console.log(filledHeaders); // in case you want to inspect
Object.assign(req.headers, filledHeaders);

// connect to the service
const bupa = await cds.connect.to('API_BUSINESS_PARTNER');

// send the request with the custom headers
const result = await bupa.send('POST', 'A_BusinessPartner', req.data.newBupa, req.headers);
console.log(result);

Notes:

  • Keep in mind this is not a production-ready code. It can be improved (and if you know how, comment below).
  • It uses @SAP-cloud-sdk/http-client library, which is what CAP uses under the hood.
  • If you wish, it also works with Node.js https library (but not http!).
  • First you need to send a request to fetch the token. Then set it on the Cookie. Finally, send the POST with the filled headers.

 

 

May this post help you on your troubleshooting. Reply if you find any issues so I, and the community, can help.

Lucas Magrini Rigo

entry.title: How to Change Date Formats in Custom CDS Views in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Problem Statement:
In the Custom CDS View app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, there are scenarios where a report requires a column with date values to be displayed in a specific format based on business needs. However, the app does not provide a direct function to change the date format. To address this, you can leverage a calculated column and utilize predefined functions such as CONCAT and SUBSTRING. By using these functions, you can manipulate the date values into the desired format.
I will demonstrate how to achieve this using an example to address such requirements effectively.

Solution:

Scenario:
Suppose there is a column in a standard CDS view containing date values in the MM/DD/YYYY format (Figure 1.1), but you need the date to be displayed in the DD-MM-YYYY format in your report.

 


Fig 1.1

Solution:
Step 1: Create a calculative column in a custom CDS.


 




Step 2: Copy the below code and paste in the code space and click on Apply.

concat( substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7 , 2 ) , concat( '-' , concat( substring(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5 , 2 ),concat( '-' , substring( I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1 , 4 ) ) )))



Explanation of code:

The SUBSTRING function used to extracts specific parts of the string based on the start position and length.
The CONCAT function is used to combine two or more strings into a single string.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 7, 2) extracts the day (characters 7 and 2 as 7 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 5, 2) extracts the month (characters 5 and 6) as 5 is start position and 2 is the length.

SUBSTRING(I_BillingDocument.BillingDocumentDate, 1, 4) extracts the year (characters 1 through 4) as 1 is start position and 4 is the length.

The CONCAT function combines these extracted parts and inserts the '-' character as a separator between them.

Step 3: Click on Check, and once the check is complete, click on Data Browser.

 

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: How to Control Events on User Decision Before/After the Decision Made
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

There was a requirement in a project that the users wants developments before and after the users made their decisions. As the developers we decided to use the program exits in a tab of the User Decision step. In this step we can call a global class and with an interface that I am going to mention below, we can see the workitem details and we can control the start time for our development by the parameters of the interface.

In this blog I will show you how can you use the program exits in the User Decision step and what is the interface for the global class.

1. Where is the program exit and how we use it.

The program exits are in the steps of workflows that we create task inside like User Decision, Activity, Send Mail etc. I will continue on User Decision step so we can see how to control decisions before and after made.

When we double click on User Decision step we can see the Program Exits tab as show below.  

   

We need to create a Z Class and give the interface IF_SWF_IFS_WORKITEM_EXIT in the class.

1.1 How we can have benefit from the Interfaces?

There are two parameters of that interface which is; IM_EVENT_NAME and IM_WORKITEM_CONTEXT.

We can reach when the class is triggered by the IM_EVENT_NAME.

IM_EVENT_NAME contains the domain values as below;

  • BEF_CREAT     Before Creation
  • CREATED         After Creation
  • BEF_EXEC       Before Execution
  • AFT_EXEC       After Execution
  • AFT_ASYINV    After Execution of a Synchronous Object Method
  • BEF_REMOVE  Before Physical Deletion
  • STATE_CHG     After a Status Change
  • AFT_REXEC     After Rule Execution
  • AFT_ACTION   After an Action
  • BEF_ACTION   Before an Action
  • BEF_DECI        Before Execution of a User Decision 

IM_WORKITEM_CONTEXT contains another interface which is IF_WAPI_WORKITEM_CONTEXT and there are many methods that we can use for our cases.

1.2 Create your Z Class

Create a global class and give the interface to it. By doing that you will be able to reach the method EVENT_RAISED.

Make your coding inside the method and don't forget to control it by IM_EVENT_NAME.

2. Trigger The Workflow

Now we all set. Trigger your workflow by your process. Once the workflow is triggered it can be seen from transaction SWIA.

3. How can we debug the method?

When the workflow reaches the step that you implemented a program exit, the class is triggered and by the IM_EVENT_NAME we can check the time we want to control.

Place an external debug for user SAP_WFRT in the EVENT_RAISED method of Z class.

3. Find the Parameters You Need

When we are in the debug we can see the parameters as below and every step of user decision the IM_EVENT_NAME will be changed.

In the IN_WORKITEM_CONTEXT You can reach the Node ID and Task ID clearly. And inside the M_CONTAINER we can reach the Workitem ID(WI_ID).

By calling the GET_WI_CONTAINER method of the IM_WORKITEM_CONTEXT parameter we can reach the container elements that we send to the user decision.

The container elements will be shown in the MT_ELEMENTS tab.

Conclusion :

Now you can design your workflow and check the steps of the User Decision if it's on the after creation, before execution etc. and make your development.

Thanks for your time.

If you think this blog is helpful please do not forget to like and share. 🙂

entry.title: Re: How to use virtual elements into a child CDS View?
entry.content: are you showing those fields for child entity in UI?

entry.title: Switching from ALV GUI to List Report Fiori RESTful Abap Programming is now easy
entry.content:
List report read-only 

In this topic will create a simple Fiori List Report without object page. It's recommended to use odata v4 but here we will create both v2 and v4

CDS Model 

The CDS model is driven by the VDM(Virtual data model) which follow consistent modeling and naming rules.

View entity Basics/Composites

Create new data definition language in ADT(need eclipse 😀 )

DDIC view based are obsoletes, so we only use view entity.

We don't use table directly beceause it's not clean core, instead we use release CDS, you can see this if they are flagged with "#PUBLIC_LOCAL_API" or release C1 contract in API State.

Now fill your new CDS :

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS basic purchase orders'
define view entity ZI_MyPurchaseOrders
  as select from I_PurchaseOrderAPI01
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'DocumentCurrency'
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency

}

 

Compile your CDS, and if you want you can add documentation :

Now you can fill your own documentation

And if you refresh your CDS you can open your documentation or if you press F2 on your object you can see documentation with definition

Now we will create our root view entity but you can also create other CDS levels between the two.

The root view entity is the last level before projection view. We'll go into more detail in the object section on page

You can use the code below

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS root purchase orders'
define root view entity ZR_MyPurchaseOrders
  as select from ZI_MyPurchaseOrders
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency
}

 

Compile

View entity Projection View

Now you need to create the top level view. Only the fields you have in this view can be displayed.

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK
@EndUserText.label: 'CDS Projection view Purchase Orders'
define root view entity ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS
  provider contract transactional_query
  as projection on ZR_MyPurchaseOrders
{
  key PurchaseOrder,
      PurchaseOrderType,
      PurchaseOrderSubtype,
      PurchasingDocumentOrigin,
      CreatedByUser,
      CreationDate,
      PurchaseOrderDate,
      CashDiscount1Percent,
      CashDiscount1Days,
      DownPaymentAmount,
      DocumentCurrency
}

 

As you're pointing to a root cds view, you need to specify the root instruction in the projection view too.

Also you need to provide a contract, for our example it's "transactional_query", you can bypass this but it's not recommended.

Compile

Metadata Extension

The metadata extension is used to arrange elements on the UI application with annotations. You can have multiple levels of metadata extension. 

Add fields position in table

By default, no fields will be displayed. To achieve this, we're going to add annotations at the CDS level (where they can also be added in the frontend application).

So firstly you need to add this annotation in your projection view

 

@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

 

Then to add a field in first position you'll use this annotation

 

"""Metadata extension """
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 },{ importance: #HIGH }]

 

Increment from 10 to 10 to make reworking easier if you've got a position wrong.

Add selection fields(filters) position in table

 

"""Metadata extension """
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]

 

Add value helps

To add value helps your have two options, one with relationship with foreign key and the second with cds view

We focus only on the second option. To do this, we'll need a cds view of type value help, which we'll call up and which will return all the values in the database.

 

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: '/DMO/I_AGENCY_STDVH', element: 'AgencyID'  } }]

 

Create and fill your metadata extension

Now that we have the three default annotations for a correct list report, we can get started.

Then you can choose to annotate entity or view.

Choose view for example

Now the aim is to add annotations for every field you want

 

@Metadata.layer: #CUSTOMER
@UI: {
    headerInfo: {
        typeName: 'Purchase Order',
        typeNamePlural: 'Purchase Orders'
    },
    presentationVariant: [{
        sortOrder: [{
            by: 'PurchaseOrder',
            direction: #DESC
        }],
        visualizations: [{
            type: #AS_LINEITEM
        }]
    }]
}
@Search.searchable: true
annotate view ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS with
{
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 },{ importance: #HIGH }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 10 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchaseOrder;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 },{ importance: #HIGH }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity : {name: 'I_PurchasingDocumentType', element: 'PurchasingDocumentType'  } }]
PurchaseOrderType;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 30 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 30 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchaseOrderSubtype;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 40 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 40 }]
@Search.defaultSearchElement: true
@Search.fuzzinessThreshold: 0.8
PurchasingDocumentOrigin;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 50 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 50 }]
CreatedByUser;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 60 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 60 }]
CreationDate;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 70 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 70 }]
PurchaseOrderDate;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 80 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 80 }]
CashDiscount1Percent;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 90 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 90 }]
CashDiscount1Days;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 100 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 100 }]
DownPaymentAmount;
@UI.lineItem: [{ position: 110 }]
@UI.selectionField: [{ position: 110 }]
DocumentCurrency;
}

 

Business Services

Now we will define entities and create odata service

Service Definition

Create new Service Definition

Insert the code below

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Service definition for ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
define service ZUI_MYPURCHORDERS {
  expose ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS as PurchaseOrders;
}

 

Don't forget to alias your entities

Service Binding

Now we'll create our odata service. Here's how to create an odata v2 and v4 service.

You need to activate your service binding (ctrl+f3)

Now you have two way to activate your odata service, use the publish button BUT it's only for testing because the input will be local and cannot be put into a package, or use the t-code /IWFND/V4_ADMIN to transport it in package.

I advise you to check that there are no errors with the publish option in service binding, as you can sometimes get errors with conversion routines (which are not allowed in view entities with 3 exceptions). Once ok, you can unpublish it and then republish it with the transaction for productive use.

Now your odata V4 is ready to use.

For V2 it's the same logic but for productivity you need to activate with t-code /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE

 

Access control (DCL)

If you want to add authorization objects in this application, you can add access control. For example if you want restrict purchase order type with PFCG object. Once you've created your odata service, you'll need to add the authorization objects used in access control in su24.

Make sure you have enabled authorization control on the root view

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK

 

Create DCL(Access Control) for root view entity :

Then insert the code below

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Access control for ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS {
    grant 
        select
            on
                ZR_MYPURCHASEORDERS
                    where ( PurchaseOrderType ) = aspect pfcg_auth ( M_BEST_BSA,BSART,actvt = '03' );
                        
}

 

And of course, if you want to use your own specific authorization object, that's also possible.

Now create another DCL(Access Control) for projection view. This DCL will only inherit the DCL of the root view.

Make sure you have enabled authorization control on the projection view

 

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK

 

Insert the below code

 

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Access control for ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS'
@MappingRole: true
define role ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS {
    grant
        select
            on
                ZC_MYPURCHASEORDERS
                    where
                        inheriting conditions from entity ZR_MyPurchaseOrders;
                        
}

 

 

Now you just need to add yours authorizations objects in your odata service with t-code SU24, and create role in PFCG and assign to you or users.

Fiori Element

Open Business Application Studio or VScode(with configured Fiori tools extension).

Launch "Fiori open application generator" with command palette(ctrl+shift+p)

Select Fiori Element List report and select your system, odata service,project name, options

Now your app is ready to test.

You'll notice that by default it has added a navigation in manifest.json to an object page that we haven't coded in the CDS views. We'll set this aside.

    },
        "PurchaseOrdersObjectPage": {
          "type": "Component",
          "id": "PurchaseOrdersObjectPage",
          "name": "sap.fe.templates.ObjectPage",
          "options": {
            "settings": {
              "editableHeaderContent": false,
              "contextPath": "/PurchaseOrders"
            }
          }
        }

Test your app with command 

npm run start

You can also test your value help in purchase order type

And congrats, you've got a simple List Report application that respects the new development standards.

Of course, this display is very simplistic as we've added very few features, so feel free to dig deeper. You can also extend your application to add a custom filter or a new column. And if the design of the lists reports doesn't suit your needs you can always go for FPM and even freestyle(but no longer recommended 😀 )

 

Technical Catalog

If you want to deploy for an Private Cloud/On-Premise system in the Fiori Launchpad, you need to create at least a technical catalog. To do this create your catalog in t-code /UI2/FLPAM an fill your Fiori application technical data(no longer use launchpad designer please)

 

Next steps

The next step is to deploy your application on your SAP system. To do this, you'll need to run the deployment configuration (npm run deploy-config). Also you may need to fill in the object page and we'll look at that in a second part. And why not show how to add an action with behavior definition(BDEF).

entry.title: How to use the new GRC Ruleset Manager (GRAC_RULE_SET_MANAGER Sample Excel File)
entry.content:
Hi Community of SAP,

In this article, you will explore the new Ruleset Manager in SAP Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC) and learn how to effectively utilize the GRAC_RULE_SET_MANAGER sample Excel file. This comprehensive guide provides insights into configuring and managing rulesets. Whether you are an SAP administrator or a consultant, this article will equip you with the knowledge to enhance your GRC processes.

You can implement this program in sap with the links below:
3469294 Improvement note

3468630 Correction note

Thanks for your support for this process. Alican Atik (GRC Consultant)

  1. Download sample Excel Company_ARuleset.xml or create your own excel like this. (You can open with excel the xml file.)

    DOWNLOAD XML FILE (FROM GOOGLE DRIVE)
  2. Create sheets like included in sample excel file. (Or you can use for edit directly sample excel file.)

    1. Rule sets

      1. Insert Header/Headers RULE SET ID*
      2. Insert data below header whatever you want that named the rule.

    2. Rule set descriptions

      1. Insert Header/Headers RULE SET ID*, LANGUAGE*, DESCRIPTION*
      2. Insert data below header whatever you want.

    3. Risk-rule set relations

      1. Insert Header/Headers RISK ID*, RULESET ID*
      2. Insert data below header RISK ID* like XBS001 (Should start with X (It's optional but it's like common usage.)),
      3. Insert data below header RULESET ID* whatever you wanted Ruleset ID.

    4. Risks

      1. Insert Header/Headers RISK ID*, RISK LEVEL*, BUSINESS PROCESS ID*, RISK TYPE*, ACTIVE
      2. Insert data below header RISK ID*.
      3. Insert data below header RISK LEVEL* as GRACRISKLEVEL table. As you can see below customizing.
      4. Insert data below header BUSINESS PROCESS ID* as GRACBPROC table. As you can see below customizing.
      5. Insert data below header RISK TYPE*.
        You can use them as respectively:
        1- Segregation of Duties,
        2- Critical Action,
        3- Critical Permission.
      6. Insert data below header ACTIVE.
        1 is active. 0 is inactive.

    5. Risk descriptions

      1. Insert Header/Headers RISK ID*, LANGUAGE*, DESCRIPTION*, LONG DESCRIPTION, CONTROL OBJECTIVE
      2. I believe all data sections are clear for understanding. Only Control Objective side I do not know what about that. If you have any idea please write in the comment. Standart value is "-" without double quotations.

    6. Risk owners

      1. Insert Header/Headers RISK ID* RISK OWNER*
      2. I believe all data sections are clear for understanding.

    7. Risk-function relations

      1. Insert Header/Headers RISK ID* FUNCTION ID*
      2. Insert data below header RISK ID*.
      3. Insert data below header FUNCTION ID* like YBS001 (Should start with Y (It's optional but it's like common usage.))

    8. Functions

      1. Insert Header/Headers FUNCTION ID* BUSINESS PROCESS ID* SCOPE*
      2. FUNCTION ID* and BUSINESS PROCESS ID* is clear. SCOPE* side values is like:
        S- Single System
        C- Cross System

    9. Function descriptions

      1. Insert Header/Headers FUNCTION ID* LANGUAGE* DESCRIPTION*
      2. I believe all data sections are clear for understanding.

    10. Function actions

      1. Insert Header/Headers FUNCTION ID* CONNECTOR* ACTION* ACTIVE
      2. FUNCTION ID* is clear.
      3. CONNECTOR* As you can see below customizing.
      4. ACTION* is tcode. You can check on TSTC.
      5. ACTIVE values is 0 and 1.
        1 is active.
        0 is inactive.

    11. Function permissions

      1. Insert Header/Headers FUNCTION ID* CONNECTOR* ACTION* RESOURCEID* RESOURCEEXTN* FROMVAL TOVAL SEARCHTYPE* ACTIVE
      2. FUNCTION ID* is clear.
      3. CONNECTOR* as I explained.
      4. ACTION* as I explained.
      5. RESOURCEID* is authorization objects. You can check on TOBJ.
      6. FROMVAL* and TOVAL* is clear. It's values. You can check function SUSR_AUTF_GET_F4_HELP. If you want to fill single value, you can fill only FROMVAL*.
      7. SEARCHTYPE* values is AND or OR.
      8. ACTIVE* values 1 Active and 0 Inactive.

    12. Business processes

      1. As I explained.

    13. Business process descriptions

      1. It's clear as I explained.
  3. Execute SE38 and write program name GRAC_RULE_SET_MANAGER and execute.
  4. Choose File Mode and Upload. Choose whatever you want append or overwrite. (I suggest Data Inconsistency Check.)
  5. If you choose Data Inconsistency Check you may encounter popup like below.
    You can check application logs on SLG1. Example is below.
  6. After everything you can click the Execute button and choose xml file.
  7. The program has no loading screen. If your file is large you need to wait. After finish you encounter popup like below.

    In conclusion, by utilizing the new Ruleset Manager in SAP Governance, Risk, and Compliance (GRC), you can effectively establish robust risk management and compliance processes. The sample Excel file and configuration steps provided in this article are customizable to meet the specific needs of your organization. Remember, you can always seek support from community for any challenges you may encounter during this process. The steps you take to enhance your GRC practices will significantly contribute to minimizing risks and ensuring compliance.

    Thank you for your potiental participation and support. If you have any questions or feedback, please feel free to share in the comments!

entry.title: Início do Projeto 4FUTURE: Uma Nova Era para o Grupo Aço Cearense
entry.content:
É com entusiasmo que celebramos o início do Projeto 4FUTURE, a jornada do Grupo Aço Cearense para a migração do SAP ECC para o SAP S/4HANA, marcando um marco histórico na transformação digital da organização. Este projeto, que tem início em 2025, representa um passo decisivo rumo à inovação, eficiência operacional e exploração do potencial tecnológico do SAP S/4HANA.

Como a Aço Cearense sempre apoiou o evento da comunidade SAP Inside Track Fortaleza, reforçamos o desejo de que esta iniciativa seja não apenas transformadora, mas também um modelo de referência, aproveitando as mais recentes inovações tecnológicas e o vasto conhecimento da Comunidade SAP do Nordeste Brasileiro.

Estamos confiantes de que o 4FUTURE trará benefícios significativos ao Grupo Aço Cearense, consolidando sua posição como líder no setor. Que este seja um projeto guiado pela colaboração, criatividade e pelo espírito inovador que caracteriza nossa comunidade! 🚀

O grupo organizador.
